basic_details.job_title: DRAT Law Researcher Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: Debts Recovery Appellate Tribunal (DRAT) Law Researcher Notification 2026 has been released on the official website of the Debts Recovery Appellate Tribunal. A total of 4 vacancies have been announced through the DRAT Law Researcher Recruitment 2026.

qualification.details: Law Researcher: Law Graduate having a Bachelor Degree in Law (including integrated Degree Course in Law)/LLM with 55% minimum marks from any College/University/Institution established by law in India and recognized by the Bar Council of India for enrolment as an Advocate. Sound knowledge of computer application including retrieval of desired information from various search engines/processes.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Law Researcher (one each particularly in DRAT)

salary_details.details: Please refer to the official notification for salary details.

selection_process: Shortlisting of applications based on academic career and extracurricular achievements Personal Interview before Selection Committee
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for DRAT Law Researcher Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 11-05-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the DRAT Law Researcher recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 11th May 2026. Ensure your application reaches the tribunal before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What are the educational qualifications required for the Law Researcher post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor Degree in Law (including integrated Degree Course in Law) or an LLM with a minimum of 55% marks. Sound knowledge of computer applications is also essential.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available for the Law Researcher position?
faq.answer: There are a total of 6 vacancies announced for the Law Researcher post in the Debts Recovery Appellate Tribunal.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for applying to this recruitment?
faq.answer: The age limit for candidates applying for the Law Researcher position is between 21 and 30 years. Age relaxation will be applicable as per government norms.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for the DRAT Law Researcher recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q6. What is the mode of application for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process is entirely via email. Candidates need to send their complete application along with required documents to the specified email address.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Law Researcher role?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting of applications based on academic achievements and extracurricular activities, followed by a personal interview conducted by a Selection Committee.

faq.question: Q8. What is the job type for the Law Researcher position?
faq.answer: The Law Researcher position is offered on a contract basis.

faq.question: Q9. Where is the Debts Recovery Appellate Tribunal located?
faq.answer: The Debts Recovery Appellate Tribunal (DRAT) is located in Mumbai, Maharashtra, with the pincode 400005.

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates with an integrated law degree apply?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates holding an integrated Degree Course in Law are eligible to apply, provided they meet the other specified educational and percentage criteria.
